subject: Science
chapter: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral

key_points: Acids and bases can be identified using synthetic indicators like Phenolphthalein and Methyl Orange.
Neutralization is an exothermic process, meaning heat is always evolved, raising the temperature of the reaction mixture.
The nature of the salt formed during neutralization (acidic, basic, or neutral) depends on the strength of the acid and base used.
Soil pH management is crucial for agriculture; acidic soil is treated with quicklime (CaO), while basic soil is treated with organic matter.
Antacid tablets (like Milk of Magnesia) contain Magnesium Hydroxide to neutralize excess Hydrochloric acid in the stomach.
examples: Synthetic Indicator: Phenolphthalein (Colorless in acid, Pink in base)
Nature of Salts: Sodium Chloride (Neutral), Sodium Carbonate (Basic), Ammonium Chloride (Acidic)
Daily Life Neutralization: Applying calamine solution (Zinc Carbonate) on ant bites to neutralize formic acid
Factory Waste: Treated with basic substances before disposal into water bodies to protect aquatic life from acidity
formulas: Reaction Heat: $Acid + Base \rightarrow Salt + Water + Heat$ (Exothermic)
Formation of Lime Water: $CaO + H_2O \rightarrow Ca(OH)_2$ (A common base used in labs)
exam_tips: Distinguish between 'Neutral' and 'Neutralization'; one is a state, the other is a chemical process.
Note that China Rose indicator turns Magenta (dark pink) in acidic and Green in basic solutions.
When soil is too basic, organic matter is added because it releases acids that neutralize the alkalinity.
If a drop of base is added to phenolphthalein-treated acid, the pink color only appears once the acid is fully neutralized.
